title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focus Theft Detection & Auto-Recovery
desc: Multi-layer system protecting user focus from CDP/Chrome theft during ask operations. Layers: (1) prevFgHint early capture before EnsureCdpConnection, (2) SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE(8) not SW_MINIMIZE(6), (3) ForceForegroundWindow(AttachThreadInput) replacing silent-fail SetForegroundWindowRaw, (4) StartFocusWatchdog 200ms monitor-only during entire ask op, (5) RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync at each action boundary, (6) FocuslessWarningOverlay + ActionApi.OnFocusStealer prop stamp, (7) DismissDialogAsync auto-dismiss 동의/agree after file-attach + pre-send. MANDATORY: all restore paths through InputReadiness pipeline. LIVING DOCUMENT: update as each focus-theft incident is resolved.
steps: 0. [MANDATORY] ALL focus restore paths go through InputReadiness.Probe() -- OnProbeSuccess fires zoom+auto-hack. Standalone ForceForegroundWindow without InputReadiness FORBIDDEN (except RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync inner loop).
1. [MANDATORY] Restore trigger = end of each ask/CDP action only. Watchdog = monitor+notify only, no restore.
5. Action boundary restore: GuardCdpFocusTheft / LogRestoreFocus -> InputReadiness.Probe() -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
6. RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync: IME+cursor snapshot -> ForceForegroundWindow loop -> OnFocusStealer(AutoBugReport+overlay) -> restore on success

8. [DONE] DismissDialogAsync: 동의 added to button match; called after file-attach + before insert. Gemini shows copyright consent after image upload -- dismiss at upload completion is the correct trigger point.
9. [TODO] FireAutoHeal on 5s restore timeout: AutoBugReport(unrecoverable) + FireAutoHeal(focus-theft, chromeHwnd)
author: claude
